title: Creatine for vegetarians
text: Creatine helps regenerate ATP for short, high-intensity efforts and supports training and recovery. Most dietary creatine comes from meat and fish, so vegetarians and vegans tend to start with lower creatine stores in their muscles, often around 20 to 30 percent lower than omnivores. Because their baseline is lower, vegetarians usually respond more strongly to creatine supplementation. People who eat eggs and dairy sit a little higher than vegans but still below meat-eaters, and the usual dose of 3 to 5 grams a day applies.
